hypothesis: Three-seed percentile intervals are inadequate for promotion and resampling nested episode rows as independent units understates uncertainty when treatment effects vary by seed.

controls.description: Naive row and paired seed-cluster intervals are evaluated on the same simulated datasets.
Independent and hierarchical null regimes expose false-positive behavior.
The weak high-noise hierarchical regime is retained without post-hoc retuning or deletion.

gates.criterion: No three-seed cell is promotion-ready under the preregistered coverage, precision, power, false-positive, and sign-stability targets.
At least one hierarchical cell improves coverage by at least 0.05 when seed clusters, rather than nested rows, are resampled.
The weak high-noise hierarchical regime is never promotion-ready at any tested seed count.
